sentences.text: Cool.
Good morning!
Hi, how's it going?
Good, how are you?
Good.
It's a Monday.
It's a Monday.
is.
It is.
I don't.
I feel like we should have a four… a 3-day weekend every weekend.
I feel the same way.
Where do you guys live?
We are all in Wisconsin.
Okay, Wisconsin.
Yeah, I feel like I don't meet many people that live in Wisconsin.
I feel like there's not many in the industry.
Anytime I tell someone I'm from Wisconsin, and they know someone else in the industry from Wisconsin, they tell me about it, so I feel like there can't be too many of us.
Yeah.
Yeah.
I think that's probably right.
Yeah.
Have you ever been, Kimberly?
You been to Wisconsin?
I have not.
I have some cousins that live there, but, I've never been.
Excuse me.
It's like, we gotta get out here, it's a great spot.
I know!
What's, like, what is Wisconsin known for?
I know Memphis, people think of, like, barbecue, obviously Tennessee, people love Nashville.
What is Wisconsin known for?
Cheese.
Okay.
here?
Here, the pattern.
Okay.
And being really cold.
Okay, I think I do know that.
I'll have to just do, like, a deep dive on TikTok later to see what the thing to do in Wisconsin is.

I moved up here a long time ago, I wasn't born here, and I grew up in Indiana, but I was in Wisconsin, I googled what to do in Wisconsin, and everything was, like, ice fishing, snowshoeing, snowmobiling, skiing, it was all winter sports.
So… but the summers are great here.
Summers are great.
And fall.
Falls are great.
balls.
Yeah.
Are you guys experiencing the fall weather right now, or our fake fall season, or is it still summer?
It's fake fall, fully fake fall, and I thought we were… I thought we were done.
I bought some pumpkins already.
Then I looked at the weather next week, it's gonna be in the 80s again, so I'm not mad about that, but it's been nice to have windows open and candles lit.
I know, I'm so excited.
I'm ready to watch some Harry Potter and just snuggle up and get cozy.
Harry Potter.
Harry Potter to me is, like, fall, winter season.
It's spooky.
Yeah, I feel that.
It's, like, dark and moody, kind of.
I agree, you get it, you get it.
Where are you based?
I am in Memphis.
Oh, okay, Memphis, nice.
Have y'all ever been to Memphis?
I have not.
No.
I have.
My brother used to live in Marion, Arkansas, which is, like, right.
Yay!
Right across.
the border, so I've spent… Some nights on Beale Street.
Oh, 11 miles.
You are at Beale Street.
Which one?
Which one?
It's just the unpopular, and just, you know…
Oh, boy.
You gotta get… that has to happen.
It's like a rites of passage.
Yeah, so… it was quite a night.
Any follow-up questions to.
Me too.
I know, me too.
questions.
Worked with Patrick for 7 years, and I've never heard this story, so…
I was very young, just… there was an Elvis impersonator.
Doing his thing, and I just… Got kicked out, so…
I don't…
I'll leave out.
Are you harassing Elvis?
I just remember there was an Elvis impersonator.
No, I was not harassing Elvis.
I, left the bar.
And then, to go to an ATM, I had too much to drink.
I tried to… by the time I went to the bar.
and left, they had put… there was a balancer, so you could get him no cover charge, which… of which I did.
And when I left to get more cash, when I came back, there was all of a sudden a cover charge.
And I just… I just thought I could just, you know, just… just… I'm already in there, just go right on in.
And then, so, I ended up, getting myself and my party kicked out, because I was circumventing the cover charge.
Wow.
You're putting up… putting up about that cover charge.
Mind you, this was 15 years ago, so don't… don't judge… this is our first time meeting, don't… Don't, don't, you know, we've grown up since then.
I get it.
I love a good Bill Street night, so I get it.
We can just drink in the street, at least back in 15 years ago, they're just…
Yeah, no, you still can.
So, I went to college here.
I grew up in Mississippi, but I lived here, or moved here, to go to college, so… trust me, I get it.
I know.
I spent a lot of college nights on Bill Street, so I totally get it.
Good time.
I was at fault there.
I had left, then there was a cover charge, and I just… I didn't agree with the… I thought since I was already in, I should have been circumventing the cover charge, but they were telling me…
grandfathered in.
Exactly.
I get it.
For context, Paige and Kylie, if you didn't know, Bill Street is kind of like a similar concept to Bourbon Street.
Yeah.
Hmm.
Got it.
Okay, I'm picturing it now.
It makes a little bit more sense.
Yeah.
Yeah, you just walk around, it's like there's no… you just walk around, there's drinks… you can buy drinks outside of the street.
Party on the street, yeah.
Yeah, it's like a big block party, it's pretty cool.
Fun.
So that's my one Memphis, my one Memphis story.
That's, like, pretty innocent, so… it could have been significantly worse.
So, we'll take it.
agent.
to end up…
I get to feel straight, yeah.
Yeah, you do.
Yeah, I'll come visit.
Stacey is out of town this week, not sure if y'all know, but, she is on vacation.
Awesome.
I'm happy to jump in and get started.
I guess I should introduce myself now.
I'm Kimberly.
Been at Fogelman for a little over 2 years.
I oversee social, reputation.
And community rewards for all the properties.
so, yeah.
Pretty much what I do.
Cool.
Yeah, Paige and I have been working with the Fogelman account, well, working with Stacy for almost 2 years now?
Maybe a little over 2 years, and specifically working with her on our Google Business Automation tool for the last little over a year.
So, I think we met very, very briefly at the Fogelman conference.
I think so.

Paige and I were out there, so… We meet with Stacy monthly to go over Google and how it's going, and we met with her last month, and she recommended that we get with you about our social tool, so… I guess just tell us a little bit about the role you're in.
I know you said you're working mostly with social reputation.
Are you and Stacy on the same team, or what as a topic.
Yes, Stacey and I are on the same team.
We have a… You know, I'm a fairly small-sized marketing team, so we're… it's kind of divided up into three sections, so Stacy is performance marketing.
I'm on the brand and communication side, so I do a lot for our corporate Brand, as well as for the properties, and then we have our marketing technology.
kind of side of the house, which is websites, SEO, all of the things.
So we all worked… work hand-in-hand together.
I… before I started at Pogelman, my previous life was paid media, and media strategy, so…
I kinda, you know, delve into that as well, even though I'm not in… on the performance side of things.
These days, you know.
pretty much the… most of our properties post on their own.
We give them the creativity and free reign to, you know.
posts for their… for their communities.
We have a few that we have on, their social media plans, organic plans, where, you know, a particular platform or company is posting content for them.
I don't think the product is similar to… you guys, this social product based on what I was, researching online.
But a lot of times, the challenge for the teams is just getting that engagement up.
Yeah.
Posting great content, you know, they are… the teams are there to lease.
A lot of times they don't have that marketing eye, they're not in tune with what's going on, you know, with SEO and a lot of the updates on Google and things like that.
So, you know, we try our best to keep them updated on just how they should be maintaining their social platforms, but, there are a good bit of them that just need the extra support, extra help.

We also have some properties that have, like, one or two office team members, so they need that extra support as well, whereas they don't have as much time dedicated to post on social, so… A lot of different… A lot of different scenarios, you know, we just try to support those teams as best as we can, so…
Yeah.
And correct me if I'm wrong on this, I believe Stacy had told us that you guys were using Reputation.
com for scheduling outposts?
Is that right?
Okay.
And so, is it the… who is creating the content to schedule through reputation?
So the teams create their own content.
on-site.
Yeah.
Okay.
So the teams create their own content.
We also have been testing out Zumper this year as well.
Yup.
So, yeah.
Yeah, I wanna say that's… That's the only other… platform we've been using for just organic social, this year.
Okay.
Yeah, and I think she had… Stacy had told me you guys had rolled out the Zumper tool at one property?
Is that still right?
they actually, funny enough, they actually just transitioned out today.
So as of this week, I don't think we have any on Zumper anymore.

I think we have, like, two, but they both transitioned out of our portfolio, so I think, you know… We'll be searching for other properties that might need the extra support and testing out other products.
We're always looking for, you know, the best of the best.
Yeah.
So, yeah.
And was it the reels, feature for the zumper that you guys liked the most?
I think Stacy was telling us a little bit about how they had included reels in that.
How did that go?
They included… they included reels, and of course, static posts.
It's nice to have reels added into any sort of media mix.
Yep.

I think the disadvantage was that… we had not gotten out to those properties to film actual, like, video content, so the reels that they were creating were mostly just, like, slideshow reels from images,
If it works, it works, but I think in order to… get better engagement.
We… I personally would like to see just actual video content incorporated into, you know, videos and reels.
So that was the only downside, but, you know, the team… the teams at that time just needed the Extra support getting content out there in general, so we were just happy that we had that.
Help, so…
Yeah, makes sense.
Yeah, in terms of the reputation posts that you're asking the teams to schedule out, how often are they typically posting?
Like, how many times a week?
Two to three times per week.
Okay.
And the marketing team provides them, a social calendar, that they can follow, they can take inspiration from.
There are some… a lot of teams who follow it to a T, a lot of teams, actually.
And then, of course, you have some teams who just are more creative, more marketing savvy than others.
And they use that calendar and add more of their own content and creativity into their social posting as well, so…
Yeah.
And then the post types that… so the calendar is telling them, like, what post types they should be doing per week, is that right?
Yes.
You and your team?
Yep, yep, we, yeah, we do that in-house.
And the post types can range from carousels, static images, reels, of course, the focus would be, you know, awareness on the community itself, appealing to prospects.

Any sort of, like, lead generation posts where you're posting about unit availability, floor plans, and… things like that, and also, you know, resident-focused posts to get that engagement, posting about the communities, resident events, things happening in the local community, and, you know, business spotlights and things like that.
Yeah, yeah, that makes sense.
I'd say that's what we're hearing most customers saying as well, as far as, like, the variation of post types, is you want property-specific content, especially now with, Google crawling social.
alone.
important to have that property-specific, but then we're hearing a lot of that it's important to incorporate for your current community that's following you as well, so…
Yeah.
Yeah, we hear, we hear a lot of that.
Last question before I jump in and I'll show you, is what led you guys to the rollout with Reputation.
com?
So, the conversation actually started to take place a little bit before my time at Fogelman.
We were on Sochi before, I think the product itself, if I remember.
correctly was fine.
It was fine.

It was more of, a support issue that I think was lacking, Our company really values our relationship with our partners, and that was the one… one of the biggest factors in us deciding to switch over to reputation.
And I like the Reputation product.
It's been a little bumpy since the shift over to them last year.
One of the biggest standout features that they had that appealed to us was their survey tool feature.
And it sends surveys out through every touchpoint of prospect-to-resident journey, and posts that as well.
So that's something that we… we really liked, and of course, they… I've made product, upgrades.
Since then, they have product upgrades on the way, you know, incorporating more AI features, and things like that, so… So, if that answers your question.
Yeah, yes, no, it definitely does.
We've heard some of that feedback from Sochi in general, and I think… Stacy, and every customer of your size.
needs, like, better support, like, a lot of hands-on support, and I think Stacy could speak to how we supported you go all, so we're very much in the camp of the more communication, the better.
We're here to partner and make this long-term, so that… we've heard that from Sochi customers.
I did one more question around the automated post from Zumper.
So, would that be for property?

When you look at an automated solution like that, is that for properties that just haven't been posting enough, and so you want to provide them with Content, or is it just we don't have the people there, so we want automated content, or is it like a mix of both?
It's a combination of both, for sure.
Very much a combination of both.
Okay.
Yeah, to answer your question.
Also to circle back to you guys, I don't… I think I've only heard great things about you guys, so…
Good to hear.
to meet with y'all, yeah.
Yeah.
Well, what we'll show you today is a bit of a hybrid between what you're doing with Reputation and with Zumper.
So, it's automation forward.
You get automated posts, there's much more of a variety of posts, and then we also allow for a scheduling function, too.
So, what you're telling us really is what most of our customers experience.

We have a platform, we have a tool, some property teams do a great job, they're always in there, and others, it just… it doesn't get done as often, so I think this could possibly be a solution for those.
Maybe that you need more content, so…
I didn't realize you guys had, like, a, you know, scheduling tool, but I guess it makes sense.
Yep.
Yeah.
Cool, well, I'll hop in, and I will show you a little bit of what the product looks like.
So, Stacy and the team have been working with us, like we said, for a couple years, and the biggest reasons that they tell us, they're continuing to roll properties out is because of the automation.
So, just like the Google tool, this social tool is built to fully automate the process of both creating that content and scheduling out that content.

So that's why a lot of customers roll us out, is because, obviously, creating content is time-consuming, and your on-site teams have a lot of other things that they shouldn't need to be focusing on as well.
So, this takes that off their plate, to provide both property-specific content and, other post types that I'll show you as well.

And then another key reason that customers roll us out and partner with us is because we are able to syndicate directly with your feed, and that's the source of truth for all of the information that we're posting, is it's all coming directly from the feed.

So I know this was huge for Stacy, because we have access to all of your property photography, all of the amenities, all of your floor plan types, all directly from the feed, so it's really hands-off.
So we're taking the amenity-based information and photos and creating content off of that.

So it's a great way to make sure that the content we're posting is updated, because you're updating your photography and everything right in your feed, so it's really little to no work that needs to be done on your end.

So, how… how it works on top of that feed syndication is our tool will, select content, so it will go in and it will… it can read the amenity fields, and then find a matching photo to that amenity field, and then our AI tool layers on top of that to create the content.
So it's unique content that's being posted, because the content, the writing is… is really unique every day.
Property-specific, too.
So everything you see is… tied directly to the property.
There's not really any, like.
Mass posting that goes across all properties, at least in an automated fashion.
Yep.
Yeah, and then going off of what you were explaining as far as the post types that you're asking your team to create, is very similar to what we're doing.
So, our posts, we're doing it twice a week, so two times a week, we're posting property-specific content, especially to help with what's going on with SEO right now.
We want to make sure we're highlighting amenities around your property and getting those keywords in there.
And then layering on top post types like scheduling tours, highlighting your pet policy.
If there's a holiday that falls within that month, we'll be posting about that holiday.
lifestyle photography and lifestyle posts to show what it's like to live at your property.
I know that was something that you'd mentioned as well.
And then, local attractions.
So, we're also able to highlight local restaurants in the area, and post about that to show a little bit more of, like, that lifestyle.
And do you…
And would you, in this case, leave more of the hyper-local community posts up to the on-site teams themselves to post?
Let's just say they have a resident event, they wanna… post about.
It would be up to them to post that content.
It would be right now, so we do have a full, a whole dashboard experience with this as well.
Let me show you what that looks like.
So, we have a whole dashboard where your team can log in, and they can see all of the upcoming posts that the automated tool has created.
So, in here, you have a full list of properties, though if it was your on-site team having access to this, we could just make sure it was just their property that they could see.
So they would log in and see their property, all upcoming posts for the next month that are coming out.

And then in here, they can both edit the automated posts if they ever wanted to, so you can make changes here, you can swap photos out here, you can also change the date that it goes out, if they wanted to push the date.
But then they also have the ability to manually create.

So, if you're like, those are great post types for those 8, but we still want them doing community events, they can do that right in this dashboard, so they can see which days these posts are coming, and then navigate around those to create those Drop and drag the photo or video here, create the content, and either post it right away or schedule send.
Got it.
I can't remember if you mentioned this, or if you aren't going to mention it.
As of now, are the only post formats, static images that they can post through this dashboard?
And is that the only format type that you guys post?
The only format type that we're… that the automated tool…
kind of clip.
Not good.
Go ahead, I… I couldn't hear very well, but you sounded like you heard it, Kylie.
Go ahead.
Yeah.
Yeah, so the posts, that the automated tool is posting are static photos, but the post, the manual post can take videos as well.
Got it.
Got it.
So the automated post would just be one static images, no carousel?
That's right, yeah.
Got it.
Would you prefer to see that a different way?
Like, in a carousel?
Just depends.
I know, you know, sometimes we want to show off more photos of the clubhouse.
You have different angles, different shots.
Yep.
Maybe you want to spotlight the clubhouse, or the fitness center, and you want to showcase multiple shots, but…
Yep.
Yeah.
So, this tool, and just like the Google tool, this tool's built in-house.
So, I asked that question because it's really customer feedback that's gotten us to the product it is today.
Gotcha, okay.

also be part of this, is we roll it out, and then we would be meeting, like, we're meeting with Stacy, to get feedback, and, you know, if it's carousels that our customers are wanting, then we can get that to our product team and see if it's something that we could do.
Yeah, no, I think that makes sense, to…
put it in the team's idea portal, or whatever you call it, I think, you know.
people… You are going to… social these days to really get an idea of the property itself, to see exactly what it looks like for when they get there in person, what they can expect.
So I think it makes sense to include carousel.
Yeah.

And our tool will obviously go through different photography, so if, say, you have 30 pictures for the property, we'll be posting one version of the fitness center one month, and then the next month when we're posting it, our tool will grab a different photo.
So we're showing different angles of it, but yeah, for your point, it's a little bit more spread out, so… We could definitely see if that's something they could do.
I think one of the main benefits here, Kimberly, is it's automated, so it's automatically, you're gonna get 8 post types.
Yes, there's.
I came out.
but they're a different variety of posts.
It's not just about your amenities.
You have your holidays, your… tournows, your pet policies, your local spotlights.
So, I think this is first and kind of first of its kind in the industry to automate different post types.
And then what you said, if you have an on-site event.
Hypothetically, they could… go into the back end here and just load the flyer, and then create the post to send as well.

So it's kind of combining the two, where you have the automation, but then you also have the scheduling and the manual posting of videos and pictures for the team as well, if you'd prefer that route, in addition to the automated.
Gotcha.
This is a random question about the floor plan, post.
How does the… how does the… How does a platform optimize a floor plan images for social?
Because sometimes they're a little funky to post.
Does it… Make it, like, a landscape, or… Is it 4x5?
How does that work?
Paige, you may know more than me on this one.

Yeah, so we're just picking up those floor plans from your property management software, and I haven't seen any… I think, like, the same way that they're, like, formatted to come through is just how they're presented.
I haven't seen anything, like, super crazy as far as, like, people…
Okay, so they would just, like, appear as, like, landscape.
Yeah, like, it's just gonna drop in that floor plan as the image, so as long as we're receiving it correctly, we'll be able to post it correctly.
Is that a question?
And I know these are the same photos that we're pulling in for Google, because we're pulling it directly through the feed.
I think Stacy's only said good things about the post quality and the photo quality, so…
I heard that, too.
Yeah.
And we'll only do this for a floor plan that's available.
So one of the benefits of having the PM software feed is that we can see the floor plans that are available.
So this one is available, so we'll highlight it on the Oliver, for example.
Yep, and I did want to show… I have a couple examples of some of the local posts.
So, this is what they're looking like.
So, this will use Google API to pull in local restaurants, pulling in local restaurants, advertising the restaurant, and then including a photo.
We're gonna shorten up those texts.
What's on the… that's, like, getting done right now.
That's… that's very wordy, but.
AI these days, yeah.
AI loves a good long post, so… but we've shored up and made these shorter.

But the key is here, we want to highlight what's nearby, so it says, like, perfectly located within walking distance, so we want to make sure that we're highlighting a reason why potential renters will want to live at your property.
Gotcha.
What are your thoughts on this overall, and how it compares to what you guys are doing with Reputation and with Sumper?
Mmm… I think it is… appears to be a lot more efficient, I'll say that.
Yeah.
Especially because, you know, one is automated, two is pulling from the website.
in our CRM?
As opposed to… It is AI.
But I… I think with this, I see less, Friction, less hassle, less mistakes being made.
No.
You know, versus doing it manually, and we have, you know, photography and things like that uploaded in Reputation that the teams choose from, but…
You know, sometimes there's still room for mistakes.
mistakes there, but I like it, I like it.
I have… I'm… I am an internal… internal processor, and I process things slowly, so… I'm just taking it all in.
Totally fair.
But it's cool, I think it's super cool.
It definitely…
the way we're going in society, so I like it.
Yeah.
Yeah, and those are the exact reasons that customers have rolled it out, right?
It's the time-savings piece of it, but it's also the consistency piece.
So we have a lot of customers saying, like, yeah, it's time-consuming, but at the end of the day, it's just that they don't have all properties fully hitting the three times a week posting.
the two times a week.
posting, so it's also the consistency piece without having to, rely on humans to do it.
So, consistency across your profiles, consistency across post types, but then that time-saving piece, really.
Have you seen more or less, engagement.
With teams using you guys' product versus them doing it themselves?
Like, do you have any… Case studies that you've put together based on, you know, a time that they were not using it, a time that they were using the product?
You mean engagement as in, like.
Amount of content, or likes, and…
Likes.
Shares.
Yeah, likes, shares.
Clicks.
Agent.
We have… oh, go ahead, sorry.
Nope, that was it.
I think a lot of our customers measure this on the amount of content that goes out.
We don't… we're working on reporting on engagement, likes, shares.
From previous versus posts.
But for the most part, like Kylie said, a lot of folks We've had some customers tell us that they spend hours on a post, and they're pumped about it, and they get 5 likes.

It's like… That doesn't, to me, to seem like a good trade on time versus what you get, so the big key with this is the efficiency that you mentioned, Kimberly, and then the amount of content increases.
I don't know about… we don't have reporting on the amount of Likes and impressions increasing yet, though our product team is looking at reporting capabilities.
Damn.
Do you…
Y'all have a reporting dashboard.
Not for social.
We do for Google, but we're working on it for social right now, as far as, like, tracking likes and shares.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Do you have benchmarks internally of what you need to… what you're looking to hit at the property level or corporate level?
Not necessarily, because every community's social is different.
Yeah.
Every community's social is different.
So, no, not necessarily, not right now, at least.
Is there reporting asked, like, asked from leadership around social for each community?
Sometimes, I mean, our team reports on it monthly.
We shared up… Give feedback, to the teams.
On how to improve their social, how to increase engagement.
So it's included and definitely looked into on a monthly basis.
If you don't mind me asking, what's typically in that reporting?
We're building it right now, so it would be…
Yeah.
put in there.
So what's… what's covered in that reporting for social?
Yeah, so… Imp… so our… our… performance team, so Stacy's team, they're gonna look at impressions, likes, clicks, they're gonna look at amount of posts, so total posts posted for that month.
I wanna say that is it.
I don't think they look at, like,
Any rates or anything, so mostly just clicks, impressions, I'll have to look and see if they… Look at their performance reporting to see if they look at reach or anything like that.
Yeah.
Actually…
But I can email this to you when you send a follow-up, because I know that A reporting dashboard is… Super important to… to some teams, but… Yeah.
Okay.
But…
If you don't have it offhand, if you'd email it, that'd be great.
Just, we're… we have reporting.
For the Google side, clicks, calls, requests for directions, search views, everything like that.
We don't have a ton on social, and that's what we're kind of gathering what our customers would want to see.
We had a hunch it was, you know, amounts of content, posts.
questions, likes, so I don't think… I think that's something.
Nothing too crazy.

I mean, I… whenever I, you know, do my… my reports for… Our leadership, I definitely dig into engagement rate, click-through rate, and things like that, but I don't think our performance team is looking at that, deeply on a monthly basis.
Okay.
Cool, that makes sense.
Cool.
Well, any other questions on your end?
I'll follow up with an email, that has the deck that we went over.
I can include a couple, properties that have this live as well.
But any other questions that you had?
I don't think so, yeah, I would love to look at a few properties that have it live, of course, pricing.
I'll have to get with Stacy.
She knows the hot properties that… that would… That could use the support of a product like this, and maybe we can look at pricing, I guess, based on that.
But I don't think so at the moment.
Like I said, I'm… I'm processing, so, yeah.
Yeah.
Well, I can give you pricing.
So, pricing's $110 per property per month.
That's what we've offered for the Google tool, so we're gonna honor that for the social tool for Fogelman as well.
And is there one package, Jess, that you have?
That determines the amount of social posting, so there's not one… or several to choose from based on the different type of amount of posts that we could choose from.
There's just.
Right, yeah.
